content.full_text:
PY2307

D-78-1472

July 24, 1978


Honorable Frank Cliff
Judge of the Superior Court
191 North First Street
San Jose, California 95113

Dear Judge Cliff:

Thank you for your letter of July 12, 1978. If you are running unopposed and your name does not appear on a ballot, you need file only one post-election statement not later than January 31, 1979, with a closing date of December 31. The statement must cover both the primary and general election period. (See Government Code Section 84207)

Sincerely,


Mary Ann Kvasager
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

Superior Court
State of California
COUNTY OF SANTA CLARA
191 NO FIRST STREET
SAN JOSE CALIFORNIA 95113
(408) 299-1121

FRANK CLIFF, JUDGE

July 12, 1978


State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

RE:  Post-Election Campaign Statements

Gentlemen:

     The undersigned judge is in receipt of your notice
of July 7, 1978, reminding all candidates for State office,
including judges, that their campaign committees are required to file statements. Please be advised that, although
I was up for election this year, I was unopposed and therefore my name was not on the primary ballot. It is my understanding that I will be reelected automatically following
the November election.

     I did not campaign and had no campaign committee.
I did not spend any money on the election, nor did I solicit
or receive any campaign funds.

     If I am required to file a statement to this effect
on one of your forms, please furnish me with same.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        FRANK CLIFF, Judge

FC:fg
